<script>
(function (global, factory) {
    typeof exports === 'object' && typeof module !== 'undefined' ? module.exports = factory() :
    typeof define === 'function' && define.amd ? define(factory) :
    (global = global || self, global.ClipboardCopyElement = factory());
  }(this, function () { 'use strict';

    function createNode(text) {
      const node = document.createElement('pre');
      node.style.width = '1px';
      node.style.height = '1px';
      node.style.position = 'fixed';
      node.style.top = '5px';
      node.textContent = text;
      return node;
    }

    function copyNode(node) {
      if ('clipboard' in navigator) {
        // eslint-disable-next-line flowtype/no-flow-fix-me-comments
        // $FlowFixMe Clipboard is not defined in Flow yet.
        return navigator.clipboard.writeText(node.textContent);
      }

      const selection = getSelection();

      if (selection == null) {
        return Promise.reject(new Error());
      }

      selection.removeAllRanges();
      const range = document.createRange();
      range.selectNodeContents(node);
      selection.addRange(range);
      document.execCommand('copy');
      selection.removeAllRanges();
      return Promise.resolve();
    }
    function copyText(text) {
      if ('clipboard' in navigator) {
        // eslint-disable-next-line flowtype/no-flow-fix-me-comments
        // $FlowFixMe Clipboard is not defined in Flow yet.
        return navigator.clipboard.writeText(text);
      }

      const body = document.body;

      if (!body) {
        return Promise.reject(new Error());
      }

      const node = createNode(text);
      body.appendChild(node);
      copyNode(node);
      body.removeChild(node);
      return Promise.resolve();
    }

    function copy(button) {
      const id = button.getAttribute('for');
      const text = button.getAttribute('value');

      function trigger() {
        button.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('clipboard-copy', {
          bubbles: true
        }));
      }

      if (text) {
        copyText(text).then(trigger);
      } else if (id) {
        const root = 'getRootNode' in Element.prototype ? button.getRootNode() : button.ownerDocument;
        if (!(root instanceof Document || 'ShadowRoot' in window && root instanceof ShadowRoot)) return;
        const node = root.getElementById(id);
        if (node) copyTarget(node).then(trigger);
      }
    }

    function copyTarget(content) {
      if (content instanceof HTMLInputElement || content instanceof HTMLTextAreaElement) {
        return copyText(content.value);
      } else if (content instanceof HTMLAnchorElement && content.hasAttribute('href')) {
        return copyText(content.href);
      } else {
        return copyNode(content);
      }
    }

    function clicked(event) {
      const button = event.currentTarget;

      if (button instanceof HTMLElement) {
        copy(button);
      }
    }

    function keydown(event) {
      if (event.key === ' ' || event.key === 'Enter') {
        const button = event.currentTarget;

        if (button instanceof HTMLElement) {
          event.preventDefault();
          copy(button);
        }
      }
    }

    function focused(event) {
      event.currentTarget.addEventListener('keydown', keydown);
    }

    function blurred(event) {
      event.currentTarget.removeEventListener('keydown', keydown);
    }

    class ClipboardCopyElement extends HTMLElement {
      constructor() {
        super();
        this.addEventListener('click', clicked);
        this.addEventListener('focus', focused);
        this.addEventListener('blur', blurred);
      }

      connectedCallback() {
        if (!this.hasAttribute('tabindex')) {
          this.setAttribute('tabindex', '0');
        }

        if (!this.hasAttribute('role')) {
          this.setAttribute('role', 'button');
        }
      }

      get value() {
        return this.getAttribute('value') || '';
      }

      set value(text) {
        this.setAttribute('value', text);
      }

    }

    if (!window.customElements.get('clipboard-copy')) {
      window.ClipboardCopyElement = ClipboardCopyElement;
      window.customElements.define('clipboard-copy', ClipboardCopyElement);
    }

    return ClipboardCopyElement;

  }));
</script>
<script>
      document.addEventListener('clipboard-copy', function(event) {
        const notice = event.target.querySelector('.notice')
        notice.hidden = false
        setTimeout(function() {
          notice.hidden = true
        }, 1000)
      })
</script>

<script type="text/x-mathjax-config">
    init_mathjax = function() {
        if (window.MathJax) {
        // MathJax loaded
            MathJax.Hub.Config({
                TeX: {
                    equationNumbers: {
                    autoNumber: "AMS",
                    useLabelIds: true
                    }
                },
                tex2jax: {
                    inlineMath: [ ['$','$'], ["\\(","\\)"] ],
                    displayMath: [ ['$$','$$'], ["\\[","\\]"] ],
                    processEscapes: true,
                    processEnvironments: true
                },
                displayAlign: 'center',
                messageStyle: 'none',
                CommonHTML: {
                    linebreaks: {
                    automatic: true
                    }
                }
            });

            MathJax.Hub.Queue(["Typeset", MathJax.Hub]);
        }
    }
    init_mathjax();

<!-- End of mathjax configuration --><script type="module">
  document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", async () => {
    const diagrams = document.querySelectorAll(".jp-Mermaid > pre.mermaid");
    // do not load mermaidjs if not needed
    if (!diagrams.length) {
      return;
    }
    const mermaid = (await import("https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/mermaid/11.10.0/mermaid.esm.min.mjs")).default;
    const elkUrl = "https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/mermaid-layout-elk/0.1.9/mermaid-layout-elk.esm.min.mjs";
    if(elkUrl) {
      const elkLayouts = (await import(elkUrl)).default;
      mermaid.registerLayoutLoaders(elkLayouts);
    }
    const parser = new DOMParser();

    mermaid.initialize({
      maxTextSize: 100000,
      maxEdges: 100000,
      startOnLoad: false,
      fontFamily: window
        .getComputedStyle(document.body)
        .getPropertyValue("--jp-ui-font-family"),
      theme: document.querySelector("body[data-jp-theme-light='true']")
        ? "default"
        : "dark",
    });

    let _nextMermaidId = 0;

    function makeMermaidImage(svg) {
      const img = document.createElement("img");
      const doc = parser.parseFromString(svg, "image/svg+xml");
      const svgEl = doc.querySelector("svg");
      const { maxWidth } = svgEl?.style || {};
      const firstTitle = doc.querySelector("title");
      const firstDesc = doc.querySelector("desc");

      img.setAttribute("src", `data:image/svg+xml,${encodeURIComponent(svg)}`);
      if (maxWidth) {
        img.width = parseInt(maxWidth);
      }
      if (firstTitle) {
        img.setAttribute("alt", firstTitle.textContent);
      }
      if (firstDesc) {
        const caption = document.createElement("figcaption");
        caption.className = "sr-only";
        caption.textContent = firstDesc.textContent;
        return [img, caption];
      }
      return [img];
    }

    async function makeMermaidError(text) {
      let errorMessage = "";
      try {
        await mermaid.parse(text);
      } catch (err) {
        errorMessage = `${err}`;
      }

      const result = document.createElement("details");
      result.className = 'jp-RenderedMermaid-Details';
      const summary = document.createElement("summary");
      summary.className = 'jp-RenderedMermaid-Summary';
      const pre = document.createElement("pre");
      const code = document.createElement("code");
      code.innerText = text;
      pre.appendChild(code);
      summary.appendChild(pre);
      result.appendChild(summary);

      const warning = document.createElement("pre");
      warning.innerText = errorMessage;
      result.appendChild(warning);
      return [result];
    }

    async function renderOneMarmaid(src) {
      const id = `jp-mermaid-${_nextMermaidId++}`;
      const parent = src.parentNode;
      let raw = src.textContent.trim();
      const el = document.createElement("div");
      el.style.visibility = "hidden";
      document.body.appendChild(el);
      let results = null;
      let output = null;
      try {
        let { svg } = await mermaid.render(id, raw, el);
        svg = cleanMermaidSvg(svg);
        results = makeMermaidImage(svg);
        output = document.createElement("figure");
        results.map(output.appendChild, output);
      } catch (err) {
        parent.classList.add("jp-mod-warning");
        results = await makeMermaidError(raw);
        output = results[0];
      } finally {
        el.remove();
      }
      parent.classList.add("jp-RenderedMermaid");
      parent.appendChild(output);
    }


    /**
     * Post-process to ensure mermaid diagrams contain only valid SVG and XHTML.
     */
    function cleanMermaidSvg(svg) {
      svg = svg.replace(RE_VOID_ELEMENT, replaceVoidElement);
      return `${SVG_XML_HEADER}${svg}`;
    }


    /**
     * A regular expression for all void elements, which may include attributes and
     * a slash.
     *
     * @see https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Void_element
     *
     * Of these, only `<br>` is generated by Mermaid in place of `\n`,
     * but _any_ "malformed" tag will break the SVG rendering entirely.
     */
    const RE_VOID_ELEMENT =
      /<\s*(area|base|br|col|embed|hr|img|input|link|meta|param|source|track|wbr)\s*([^>]*?)\s*>/gi;

    /**
     * Ensure a void element is closed with a slash, preserving any attributes.
     */
    function replaceVoidElement(match, tag, rest) {
      rest = rest.trim();
      if (!rest.endsWith('/')) {
        rest = `${rest} /`;
      }
      return `<${tag} ${rest}>`;
    }

<h1 id="model-construction-and-comparison">Model Construction and Comparison<a class="anchor-link" href="#model-construction-and-comparison">¶</a></h1><p>For this phase of the project, we now proceeed onto constructing unsupervised learning models based on <strong>K-Means Clustering</strong> and <strong>Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering</strong>, both models are fundamentally different, and therefore, this present phase of the project will have the main focus of constructing several models and choosing the best out of each one of the models for clustering the previously worked on dataset, thereby selecting an adequate amount of clusters, which are then able to offer valuable insight into GBM subtypes.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="jp-Cell jp-MarkdownCell jp-Notebook-cell" id="cell-id=80743b65">
<div class="jp-Cell-inputWrapper" tabindex="0">
<div class="jp-Collapser jp-InputCollapser jp-Cell-inputCollapser">
</div>
<div class="jp-InputArea jp-Cell-inputArea"><div class="jp-InputPrompt jp-InputArea-prompt">
</div><div class="jp-RenderedHTMLCommon jp-RenderedMarkdown jp-MarkdownOutput" data-mime-type="text/markdown">
<h2 id="k-means-clustering">K-Means Clustering<a class="anchor-link" href="#k-means-clustering">¶</a></h2><p>K-Means was selected as the principal model to identify transcriptomic subtypes. Because the dataset was transformed via PCA into a Euclidian space, K-Means is well suited to partition the variance. To prevent the convergence of centroids on non-ideal local minima, the <code>k-means++</code> inicialization parameter was enforced when calling the sklearn implementation of K-means, this ensures centroids are distributed with a maximum based distance. The algorithm was iteratively evaluated across 2-6 clusters to empirically determine the optimal cluster count without biological bias, due to the extensive research on Glioblastoma showing there are 4 main subtypes of the ailment (Classical, Mesenchymal, Proneural and Neural) (Can Xu, et al., 2024).</p>
<p>For <code>sklearn.cluster.KMeans</code>, the following parameters were used to train the model:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>n_clusters</code>: The numebr of centroids. This will loop from 2 to 6.</li>
<li><code>init='k-means++</code>: Standard K-Means drop centroids randomly, this places the initial centroids as far away from each other, ensuring an optimized start.</li>
<li><code>n_init=10</code>: Runs the algorithm from scratch 10 different times with different starting seeds, returns the best result.</li>
<li><code>random_state</code>: Locks seed so we can reproduce the results.</li>
</ul>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div><div class="jp-Cell jp-CodeCell jp-Notebook-cell jp-mod-noOutputs">

    <span class="c1"># Model: K-Means</span>
    <span class="n">kmeans</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">KMeans</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">n_clusters</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">k</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">init</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'k-means++'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">n_init</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'auto'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">random_state</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">42</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="n">km_labels</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">kmeans</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">fit_predict</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">df_clustering_ready</span><span class="p">)</span>
    
    <span class="n">km_silhouette</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">append</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">silhouette_score</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">df_clustering_ready</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">km_labels</span><span class="p">))</span>
    <span class="n">km_db_score</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">append</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">davies_bouldin_score</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">df_clustering_ready</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">km_labels</span><span class="p">))</span>

<div class="clipboard-copy-txt" id="cell-1">import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
from sklearn.metrics import silhouette_score, davies_bouldin_score

df_clustering_ready = pd.read_csv('../../data/star_data/pca_df.csv', index_col=0)

# --- Define the Sweep Parameters ---
k_range = range(2, 7)

# Storage for K-Means metrics
km_silhouette = []
km_db_score = []

# --- Execute the K-Sweep ---
for k in k_range:
    # Model: K-Means
    kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=k, init='k-means++', n_init='auto', random_state=42)
    km_labels = kmeans.fit_predict(df_clustering_ready)
    
    km_silhouette.append(silhouette_score(df_clustering_ready, km_labels))
    km_db_score.append(davies_bouldin_score(df_clustering_ready, km_labels))</div>
</div>
</div>

</div>

<div class="jp-Cell jp-MarkdownCell jp-Notebook-cell" id="cell-id=d12864e0">
<div class="jp-Cell-inputWrapper" tabindex="0">
<div class="jp-Collapser jp-InputCollapser jp-Cell-inputCollapser">
</div>
<div class="jp-InputArea jp-Cell-inputArea"><div class="jp-InputPrompt jp-InputArea-prompt">
</div><div class="jp-RenderedHTMLCommon jp-RenderedMarkdown jp-MarkdownOutput" data-mime-type="text/markdown">
<h2 id="agglomerative-hierarchical-clustering">Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering<a class="anchor-link" href="#agglomerative-hierarchical-clustering">¶</a></h2><p>While K-Means assumes spherical, evenly distributed clusters, Glioblastoma subtypes often, as we have seen, uneven sample sizes and complex geometries. Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering was implemented as a key model in this research because its bottom-up clustering via the generation of a <em>Dendrogram</em> is able to handle unequal subtype sizes, as its fundamental way of operating makes it so that it explicitly minimizes within-cluster variance at each merge step, ensuring maximum cohesion within the resulting GBM phenotype clusters.</p>
<p>The crucial parameters used for the <code>sklearn.cluster.AgglomerativeClustering</code> library are:</p>
<ul>
<li><code>n_clusters</code>: Number of clusters to generate based on horizontal cut of dendrogram.</li>
<li><code>metric=euclidian</code>: The way to measure space between samples and generate linkages through dendrograms.</li>
<li><code>linkage=ward</code>: Most important parameter. After evaluating every linkage method available (single, complete, average and Ward's) for Hierarchical Clustering, Ward's linkage method stood out as the linkage method that generated the best results. Ward's linkage merges clusters in a way that minimizes the total within-cluster sum of squares when merging two clusters, it also tends to yield the tightest, most highly separated clusters (Scipy, n.d.), which was evident in a heuristical metric comparison which will be discussed later on.</li>
</ul>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div><div class="jp-Cell jp-CodeCell jp-Notebook-cell">
<div class="jp-Cell jp-CodeCell jp-Notebook-cell" id="cell-id=04ab57ac">
<div class="jp-Cell-inputWrapper" tabindex="0">
<div class="jp-Collapser jp-InputCollapser jp-Cell-inputCollapser">
</div>
<div class="jp-InputArea jp-Cell-inputArea">
<div class="jp-InputPrompt jp-InputArea-prompt">In [2]:</div><div class="jp-CodeMirrorEditor jp-Editor jp-InputArea-editor" data-type="inline">

<div class="clipboard-copy-txt" id="cell-2">from sklearn.cluster import AgglomerativeClustering
from sklearn.metrics import silhouette_score, davies_bouldin_score
from scipy.cluster.hierarchy import dendrogram, linkage

# Storage for Hierarchical metrics
hc_silhouette = []
hc_db_score = []

# --- 2. Execute the Dual-Model Sweep ---
for k in k_range:
    # Model B: Agglomerative Hierarchical (Ward's Linkage)
    hc = AgglomerativeClustering(n_clusters=k, metric='euclidean', linkage='ward')
    hc_labels = hc.fit_predict(df_clustering_ready)
    
    hc_silhouette.append(silhouette_score(df_clustering_ready, hc_labels))
    hc_db_score.append(davies_bouldin_score(df_clustering_ready, hc_labels))
    
# --- 4. Generate the Biological Dendrogram ---
plt.figure(figsize=(15, 7))
# Linkage matrix required for scipy's dendrogram
Z = linkage(df_clustering_ready, method='ward', metric='euclidean')

dendrogram(
    Z,
    truncate_mode='lastp',  # Show only the last p merged clusters
    p=30,                   # Show top 30 branches to keep it readable
    leaf_rotation=90.,
    leaf_font_size=10.,
    show_contracted=True,
    color_threshold=0      
)

plt.title('Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram', fontsize=16)
plt.xlabel('Patient Clusters (Truncated for Visibility)', fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel('Euclidean Distance (Ward Linkage)', fontsize=12)
plt.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)
plt.show()

<h2 id="model-evaluation-and-cross-comparison">Model Evaluation and Cross-Comparison<a class="anchor-link" href="#model-evaluation-and-cross-comparison">¶</a></h2><p>In unsupervised clustering, the absence of ground-truth Y-labels necessitates the use of internal mathematical heuristics to evaluate cluster quality and determine the optimal number of subtypes (K). To ensure mathematical rigor and prevent metric-specific bias, two distinct validation indices were employed to evaluate both the K-Means and Agglomerative Hierarchical models: <strong>the Silhouette Score (Kalai, et al., 2017) and the Davies-Bouldin Index (Chicco, et al., 2026).</strong></p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Silhouette Score (Measuring Distinctness)</strong>: The Silhouette Score was selected as the primary metric to evaluate global cluster separation. It calculates the mean intra-cluster distance and compares it to the mean nearest-cluster distance. In the context of high-dimensional RNAseq data, maximizing this score ensures that the identified GBM subtypes are fundamentally distinct transcriptomic clusters rather than arbitrary statistical partitions. In simple terms, the higher this metric the better.</li>
<li><strong>Davies-Bouldin Index (Measuring Compactness)</strong>: To validate the Silhouette findings, the Davies-Bouldin (DB) Index was implemented as a secondary heuristic. The DB index calculates the worst-case ratio of within-cluster scatter to between-cluster separation. Because it evaluates the average similarity between each cluster and its most similar neighbor, minimizing the DB score mathematically guarantees that the clusters are tightly packed and non-overlapping. In other words, the lower this metric the better.</li>
</ol>
<p>Relying on a single internal metric in high-dimensional Euclidean space is mathematically risky, as certain metrics bias toward specific geometric shapes (e.g., K-Means forcing spherical clusters). By running a dual-metric sweep, the validity of the final model is proven through consensus. The Agglomerative Hierarchical model explicitly outperformed K-Means across both heuristics, achieving a maximum Silhouette Score and a minimum Davies-Bouldin Index simultaneously at K=4. This dual-metric agreement provides robust mathematical proof for the existence of four distinct transcriptomic subtypes, perfectly aligning with established biological literature for Glioblastoma. However, Agglomerative clustering (K=4) successfully partitioned the cohort, revealing two dominant transcriptomic subtypes alongside two highly distinct, rare outlier groups (N=5, N=1). To ensure strict statistical power and prevent variance errors during the next discovery phase of the project, <strong>subsequent expression analysis was isolated to the two primary clusters found in the model.</strong></p>

<div class="highlight-ipynb hl-python"><pre><span></span><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">sklearn.metrics</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">silhouette_score</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">davies_bouldin_score</span>

<span class="c1"># --- 3. Visualize the Head-to-Head Heuristics ---</span>
<span class="n">fig</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">axes</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">plt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">subplots</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">figsize</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">15</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="mi">5</span><span class="p">))</span>

<span class="n">axes</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">set_title</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">'Silhouette Score Comparison'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">fontsize</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">14</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">axes</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">set_xlabel</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">'Number of Clusters (K)'</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">axes</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">set_ylabel</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">'Silhouette Score'</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1"># Plot B: Davies-Bouldin Index </span>
<span class="n">axes</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">plot</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">k_range</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">km_db_score</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">marker</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'o'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">color</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'#3498db'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">linewidth</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">label</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'K-Means'</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">axes</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">plot</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">k_range</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">hc_db_score</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">marker</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'s'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">color</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'#e74c3c'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">linewidth</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">label</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'Hierarchical'</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="n">axes</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">set_xlabel</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">'Number of Clusters (K)'</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">axes</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">set_ylabel</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">'Davies-Bouldin Score'</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">axes</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">legend</span><span class="p">()</span>

<span class="c1"># --- The Final Visual Dendrogram ---</span>

<span class="p">)</span>

<span class="n">plt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">title</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">'Glioblastoma Transcriptomic Lineage'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">fontsize</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">16</span><span class="p">)</span>

)

<h2 id="cluster-interpretation-gene-analysis">Cluster Interpretation - Gene Analysis<a class="anchor-link" href="#cluster-interpretation-gene-analysis">¶</a></h2><p>While PCA successfully identified the geometric boundaries of the Glioblastoma subtypes, Principal Components are abstract linear combinations of features. To extract actionable biological meaning, the PCA-derived cluster labels were mapped back to the original log-transformed gene expression dataset to perform Differential Expression (DE) analysis.</p>
<p><strong>Statistical Methodology (Mann-Whitney U Test)</strong>
Bulk RNA-sequencing data frequently violates the assumption of perfect normal distribution due to technical noise and biological outliers (Dehaghi, et al., 2025). Therefore, the non-parametric Mann-Whitney U test was selected over a standard independent t-test. This ensures that the identification of significantly expressed genes is robust against extreme outliers within the dataset.</p>
<p><strong>Magnitude Assessment (Log2 Fold Change)</strong>
Statistical significance (P-value) alone is insufficient to define a biomarker, as a gene may be statistically significant but exhibit a biologically negligible difference. To resolve this, Log2 Fold Change (Log2FC) was calculated for every gene. This dual-metric approach ensures that the final identified biomarkers are both statistically significant (p < 0.05) and biologically highly impactful, successfully isolating the primary genetic drivers of each tumor subtype.</p>
<p>To synthesize the results of the differential expression analysis, a Volcano Plot was generated to simultaneously visualize statistical significance and biological magnitude, this is the gold standard for showing in a visual way the most significant genes which differentiate the dataset in the previously generated clusters (Doyle, M., 2026). Furthermore, in order to analyze exactly which gene expressions are the ones significant enough and biologically impactful, the <code>mygene</code> library, which represents an extensive database with gene expression names and symbols. Based on the <em>NCBI Entrez Gene IDs</em>, which is the standard ID representation for TCGA gene expression data, the processed data has been crossed referrenced with the <code>mygene</code> library to extract gene expression names for further interpretation.</p>

<div class="highlight-ipynb hl-python"><pre><span></span><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">scipy.stats</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">mannwhitneyu</span>
<span class="kn">import</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">mygene</span>

<span class="c1"># --- 1. ALIGN THE DATA ---</span>
<span class="n">df_log_filtered</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">pd</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">read_csv</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">'../../data/star_data/log_transformed_dataset.csv'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">index_col</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">df_biomarker</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">df_log_filtered</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">copy</span><span class="p">()</span>

<span class="n">df_biomarker</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'Subtype'</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">df_clustering_ready</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'Transcriptomic_Subtype'</span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="n">group_0</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">df_biomarker</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">df_biomarker</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'Subtype'</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">==</span> <span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">drop</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">columns</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'Subtype'</span><span class="p">])</span>
<span class="n">group_1</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">df_biomarker</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">df_biomarker</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'Subtype'</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">==</span> <span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">drop</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">columns</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'Subtype'</span><span class="p">])</span>

<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="sa">f</span><span class="s2">"Comparing Subtype 0 (</span><span class="si">{</span><span class="nb">len</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">group_0</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="si">}</span><span class="s2"> patients) vs Subtype 1 (</span><span class="si">{</span><span class="nb">len</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">group_1</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="si">}</span><span class="s2"> patients)..."</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1"># --- 2. RUN THE DIFFERENTIAL EXPRESSION TEST ---</span>
<span class="n">results</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="p">[]</span>

<span class="k">for</span> <span class="n">gene</span> <span class="ow">in</span> <span class="n">group_0</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">columns</span><span class="p">:</span>
    <span class="c1"># Run the non-parametric Mann-Whitney U test</span>
    <span class="n">stat</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">p_val</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">mannwhitneyu</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">group_0</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">gene</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">group_1</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">gene</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">alternative</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'two-sided'</span><span class="p">)</span>
    
    <span class="c1"># Calculate the Log2 Fold Change </span>
    <span class="n">log2fc</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">group_1</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">gene</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">mean</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="o">-</span> <span class="n">group_0</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">gene</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">mean</span><span class="p">()</span>
    
    <span class="n">results</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">append</span><span class="p">({</span>
        <span class="s1">'Gene'</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="nb">str</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">gene</span><span class="p">),</span> <span class="c1"># Force string for the mygene query</span>
        <span class="s1">'P-Value'</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="n">p_val</span><span class="p">,</span>
        <span class="s1">'Log2FC'</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="n">log2fc</span>
    <span class="p">})</span>

<span class="c1"># --- 3. FORMAT INITIAL RESULTS ---</span>
<span class="n">df_results</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">pd</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">DataFrame</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">results</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">df_results</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">df_results</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">dropna</span><span class="p">()</span>

<span class="c1"># --- 4. INTEGRATE MYGENE FOR ALL GENES ---</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">"</span><span class="se">\n</span><span class="s2">Querying mygene database for 10,000 genes..."</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">mg</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">mygene</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">MyGeneInfo</span><span class="p">()</span>

<span class="c1"># Query the database</span>
<span class="n">gene_info</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">mg</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">querymany</span><span class="p">(</span>
    <span class="n">df_results</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'Gene'</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tolist</span><span class="p">(),</span> 
    <span class="n">scopes</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'entrezgene'</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="n">fields</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'symbol,name'</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="n">species</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'human'</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="n">as_dataframe</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">True</span>
<span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1"># Reset index to make merging clean</span>
<span class="n">gene_info</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">gene_info</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">reset_index</span><span class="p">()</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">rename</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">columns</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="p">{</span><span class="s1">'query'</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="s1">'Gene'</span><span class="p">})</span>

<span class="c1"># Drop any duplicate hits the API might return for a single ID</span>
<span class="n">gene_info</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">gene_info</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">drop_duplicates</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">subset</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'Gene'</span><span class="p">])</span>

<span class="c1"># Merge the biological names back into our statistical results</span>
<span class="n">df_results</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">df_results</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">merge</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">gene_info</span><span class="p">[[</span><span class="s1">'Gene'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">'symbol'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">'name'</span><span class="p">]],</span> <span class="n">on</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'Gene'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">how</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'left'</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1"># Clean up column order and handle missing symbols (some obscure IDs might not have symbols)</span>
<span class="n">df_results</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'symbol'</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">df_results</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'symbol'</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">fillna</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">'UNKNOWN'</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">df_results</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'name'</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">df_results</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'name'</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">fillna</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">'Unknown or deprecated gene'</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">df_results</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">df_results</span><span class="p">[[</span><span class="s1">'Gene'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">'symbol'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">'name'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">'P-Value'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">'Log2FC'</span><span class="p">]]</span>

<span class="c1"># Sort by the most statistically significant genes</span>
<span class="n">df_results</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">df_results</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">sort_values</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">'P-Value'</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1"># --- 5. DISPLAY TOP 20 ---</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">"</span><span class="se">\n</span><span class="s2">--- TOP 20 BIOMARKER GENES DEFINING THE SUBTYPES ---"</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">df_results</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">head</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">20</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">to_string</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">index</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">False</span><span class="p">))</span>
</pre></div>
<div class="clipboard-copy-txt" id="cell-4">from scipy.stats import mannwhitneyu
import mygene

# --- 1. ALIGN THE DATA ---
df_log_filtered = pd.read_csv('../../data/star_data/log_transformed_dataset.csv', index_col=0)
df_biomarker = df_log_filtered.copy()

df_biomarker['Subtype'] = df_clustering_ready['Transcriptomic_Subtype']
group_0 = df_biomarker[df_biomarker['Subtype'] == 0].drop(columns=['Subtype'])
group_1 = df_biomarker[df_biomarker['Subtype'] == 1].drop(columns=['Subtype'])

print(f"Comparing Subtype 0 ({len(group_0)} patients) vs Subtype 1 ({len(group_1)} patients)...")

# --- 2. RUN THE DIFFERENTIAL EXPRESSION TEST ---
results = []

for gene in group_0.columns:
    # Run the non-parametric Mann-Whitney U test
    stat, p_val = mannwhitneyu(group_0[gene], group_1[gene], alternative='two-sided')
    
    # Calculate the Log2 Fold Change 
    log2fc = group_1[gene].mean() - group_0[gene].mean()
    
    results.append({
        'Gene': str(gene), # Force string for the mygene query
        'P-Value': p_val,
        'Log2FC': log2fc
    })

# --- 3. FORMAT INITIAL RESULTS ---
df_results = pd.DataFrame(results)
df_results = df_results.dropna()

# --- 4. INTEGRATE MYGENE FOR ALL GENES ---
print("\nQuerying mygene database for 10,000 genes...")
mg = mygene.MyGeneInfo()

# Query the database
gene_info = mg.querymany(
    df_results['Gene'].tolist(), 
    scopes='entrezgene', 
    fields='symbol,name', 
    species='human', 
    as_dataframe=True
)

# Reset index to make merging clean
gene_info = gene_info.reset_index().rename(columns={'query': 'Gene'})

# Drop any duplicate hits the API might return for a single ID
gene_info = gene_info.drop_duplicates(subset=['Gene'])

# Merge the biological names back into our statistical results
df_results = df_results.merge(gene_info[['Gene', 'symbol', 'name']], on='Gene', how='left')

# Clean up column order and handle missing symbols (some obscure IDs might not have symbols)
df_results['symbol'] = df_results['symbol'].fillna('UNKNOWN')
df_results['name'] = df_results['name'].fillna('Unknown or deprecated gene')
df_results = df_results[['Gene', 'symbol', 'name', 'P-Value', 'Log2FC']]

# Sort by the most statistically significant genes
df_results = df_results.sort_values('P-Value')

# --- 5. DISPLAY TOP 20 ---
print("\n--- TOP 20 BIOMARKER GENES DEFINING THE SUBTYPES ---")
print(df_results.head(20).to_string(index=False))
</div>

<p>During the differential expression analysis, several features exhibited high statistical significance but mapped to "Unknown" or "Deprecated" Entrez IDs. This is a common artifact in retrospective RNAseq analysis, caused by database drift between the original TCGA genome assembly and current NCBI annotations (e.g., historical IDs being reclassified as non-coding fragments or retired entirely).</p>
<p>Because the objective of this clustering analysis is to identify actionable clinical biomarkers, all uncharacterized and deprecated features were filtered out of the final results. The clinical interpretation was strictly isolated to highly significant, fully annotated protein-coding genes (such as KEAP1, TFAM, and NPR2), ensuring the transcriptomic profiles are grounded in established tumor biology.</p>

<p>The differential expression analysis successfully isolated a subset of highly significant protein genes. Notably, all top 10 biomarkers (including NPR2, TFAM, KEAP1, and UBTF) exhibited a negative Log2 Fold Change, indicating substantial upregulation in cluster 0 compared to cluster 1.</p>
<p>These findings mirror established RNAseq literature, which demonstrates that Glioblastoma is not a single disease, but a collection of distinct molecular subtypes driven by different metabolic and regulatory hijackings (Verhaak et al., 2010).</p>

<span class="n">fc_threshold</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="mf">0.5</span>

<span class="c1"># Create a categorization column for coloring the dots</span>

    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">row</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'nlog10_P'</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o"><</span> <span class="n">p_threshold</span><span class="p">:</span>
        <span class="k">return</span> <span class="s1">'Not Significant'</span>
    <span class="k">elif</span> <span class="n">row</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'Log2FC'</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">></span> <span class="n">fc_threshold</span><span class="p">:</span>
        <span class="k">return</span> <span class="s1">'Upregulated in Subtype 1'</span>
    <span class="k">elif</span> <span class="n">row</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'Log2FC'</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o"><</span> <span class="o">-</span><span class="n">fc_threshold</span><span class="p">:</span>
        <span class="k">return</span> <span class="s1">'Upregulated in Subtype 0'</span>
    <span class="k">else</span><span class="p">:</span>
        <span class="k">return</span> <span class="s1">'Significant but Low Magnitude'</span>

<span class="n">df_interpret</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">'Significance'</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">df_interpret</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">apply</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">categorize_gene</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">axis</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="n">sns</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">scatterplot</span><span class="p">(</span>
    <span class="n">data</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">df_interpret</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="n">x</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'Log2FC'</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="n">y</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'nlog10_P'</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="n">hue</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'Significance'</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="n">palette</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">colors</span><span class="p">,</span>
    <span class="n">alpha</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mf">0.7</span><span class="p">,</span> 
    <span class="n">edgecolor</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">None</span><span class="p">,</span>
    <span class="n">s</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">40</span>
<span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1"># Draw the threshold lines</span>
<span class="n">plt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">axhline</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">y</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">p_threshold</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">color</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'k'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">linestyle</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'--'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">linewidth</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">alpha</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mf">0.5</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">plt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">axvline</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">x</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">fc_threshold</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">color</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'k'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">linestyle</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'--'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">linewidth</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">alpha</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mf">0.5</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="n">plt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">axvline</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">x</span><span class="o">=-</span><span class="n">fc_threshold</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">color</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'k'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">linestyle</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'--'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">linewidth</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">alpha</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="mf">0.5</span><span class="p">)</span>

<div class="clipboard-copy-txt" id="cell-6"># 1. Prepare the Data for the Volcano Plot
df_interpret['nlog10_P'] = -np.log10(df_interpret['P-Value'] + 1e-300)

# 2. Define strict thresholds for what counts as a Significant Biomarker
p_threshold = -np.log10(0.01)
fc_threshold = 0.5

# Create a categorization column for coloring the dots
def categorize_gene(row):
    if row['nlog10_P'] < p_threshold:
        return 'Not Significant'
    elif row['Log2FC'] > fc_threshold:
        return 'Upregulated in Subtype 1'
    elif row['Log2FC'] < -fc_threshold:
        return 'Upregulated in Subtype 0'
    else:
        return 'Significant but Low Magnitude'

df_interpret['Significance'] = df_interpret.apply(categorize_gene, axis=1)

# 3. Generate the Volcano Plot
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 8))

# Scatter plot with custom colors
colors = {'Not Significant': 'grey', 
          'Significant but Low Magnitude': 'lightblue', 
          'Upregulated in Subtype 1': '#e74c3c',  # Red
          'Upregulated in Subtype 0': '#2ecc71'}  # Green

sns.scatterplot(
    data=df_interpret, 
    x='Log2FC', 
    y='nlog10_P', 
    hue='Significance', 
    palette=colors,
    alpha=0.7, 
    edgecolor=None,
    s=40
)

# Draw the threshold lines
plt.axhline(y=p_threshold, color='k', linestyle='--', linewidth=1, alpha=0.5)
plt.axvline(x=fc_threshold, color='k', linestyle='--', linewidth=1, alpha=0.5)
plt.axvline(x=-fc_threshold, color='k', linestyle='--', linewidth=1, alpha=0.5)

# Formatting
plt.title('Volcano Plot: Differential Gene Expression (Subtype 0 vs Subtype 1)', fontsize=16)
plt.xlabel('Log2 Fold Change (Magnitude of Difference)', fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel('-Log10 P-Value (Statistical Significance)', fontsize=12)
plt.legend(title='Gene Status')
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.2)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()</div>

<p>The resulting Volcano Plot reveals a highly asymmetric transcriptomic divergence between the two primary Glioblastoma subsets. The dense distribution of significant features in the negative Log_2 Fold Change quadrant (green) indicates that Subtype 0 is a highly transcriptionally active state compared to Subtype 1. The extraction of specific, fully annotated protein-coding genes from this analysis confirms that these clusters are driven by genuine biological mechanisms rather than statistical artifacts. For instance, the significant differential expression of genes related to oxidative stress (e.g., KEAP1), mitochondrial regulation (TFAM), and cellular signaling (NPR2) suggests that Subtype 0 and Subtype 1 operate under fundamentally distinct metabolic and regulatory paradigms (TCGA, 2026).</p>
</div>

<h2 id="clinical-analysis">Clinical analysis<a class="anchor-link" href="#clinical-analysis">¶</a></h2><p>Unsupervised clustering is only clinically valuable if the mathematically derived subtypes correlate with distinct patient phenotypes or prognostic outcomes. To validate the clinical relevance of the transcriptomic subtypes, the cluster labels (Subtype 0 and Subtype 1) were integrated with the cohort's clinical and survival metadata obtained from USCS Xena original repository.</p>
<p>A comparative analysis was designed to evaluate whether these molecular subtypes manifest differently across demographic categories (e.g., gender), age at diagnosis (numerical), and overall survival time. Establishing a correlation between the RNA-seq clusters and these clinical variables proves that the transcriptomic divergence directly impacts patient prognosis and disease presentation.</p>
</div>

<div class="clipboard-copy-txt" id="cell-7">df_pheno = pd.read_csv('../../data/phenotype_data/TCGA-GBM.clinical.tsv', sep=None, engine='python')
df_survival = pd.read_csv('../../data/phenotype_data/TCGA-GBM.survival.tsv', sep=None, engine='python')

df_pheno.rename(columns={df_pheno.columns[0]: 'sample'}, inplace=True)
df_survival.rename(columns={df_survival.columns[0]: 'sample'}, inplace=True)
df_pheno = df_pheno.set_index('sample')
df_survival = df_survival.set_index('sample')


df_labels = df_clustering_ready[['Transcriptomic_Subtype']].copy()
clinical_master = df_labels.join(df_pheno, how='inner').join(df_survival, how='inner')
clinical_master = clinical_master[clinical_master['Transcriptomic_Subtype'].isin([0, 1])]

print(f"Successfully merged data for {len(clinical_master)} patients.\n")

# ---  THE CLINICAL COMPARISONS ---

# A. Categorical Variable: Gender
print("--- GENDER DISTRIBUTION ---")
gender_dist = pd.crosstab(
    clinical_master['Transcriptomic_Subtype'], 
    clinical_master['gender.demographic'], 
    normalize='index'
) * 100
print(gender_dist.round(2).astype(str) + '%')

# B. Numerical Variable: Age at Index/Diagnosis
print("\n--- AVERAGE AGE AT DIAGNOSIS ---")
age_dist = clinical_master.groupby('Transcriptomic_Subtype')['age_at_index.demographic'].mean()
print(age_dist.round(2))

# C. Prognostic Variable: Overall Survival (OS.time)
print("\n--- MEDIAN OVERALL SURVIVAL (DAYS) ---")
survival_dist = clinical_master.groupby('Transcriptomic_Subtype')['OS.time'].median()
print(survival_dist.round(2))</div>

<h3 id="demographic-divergence">Demographic Divergence:<a class="anchor-link" href="#demographic-divergence">¶</a></h3><p>The most notable clinical distinction between the clusters is demographic presentation. Subtype 0 exhibits a severe male-dominant skew (68.7% male vs. 31.3% female). On the other hand, Subtype 1 presents a much more balanced gender distribution (55.6% male vs. 44.4% female), suggesting that the underlying gene drivers of Subtype 1 may be less influenced by sex-specific biological factors. Age at diagnosis remained relatively consistent across both cohorts (58.5 years vs. 60.7 years), aligning with the standard late-onset presentation of the disease.</p>
<h3 id="survival-and-clinical-conclusion">Survival and Clinical Conclusion:<a class="anchor-link" href="#survival-and-clinical-conclusion">¶</a></h3><p>Despite operating on fundamentally different transcriptomic and metabolic paradigms, the median overall survival for both cohorts is functionally identical (357 days for Subtype 0, 360 days for Subtype 1).
Far from making the clustering process obsolete, this similarity in survival highlights the core crisis in modern Glioblastoma treatment. It demonstrates that standard-of-care therapies are probably failing on both molecular phenotypes equally. The pipeline successfully proved that these patients possess vastly different tumor biologies (e.g., the hyperactive metabolic signature of Subtype 0). The identical, poor survival rates underscore the current clinical need to abandon generalized treatments in favor of targeted therapies that specifically attack the unique biomarker profiles (such as TFAM or KEAP1 dependencies) identified in this unsupervised analysis.</p>

title: Model Construction and Comparison
